chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ الْحُلِيِّ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهَا مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – What is allowed for the female in Ihraam to wear from the clothes, and the ornaments, and what is disliked for her from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/293)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/293)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مُصَبَّغَاتُ الثِّيَابِ تَلْبَسُهُ الْمُحْرِمَةُ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ إِلَّا الْمُفْدَمَ الْمَشْهُورَ وَ الْقِلَادَةَ الْمَشْهُورَةَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Safwan, from Hareyz, from Aamir Bin Juza’at who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘The dyed clothes, can the female in Ihraam wear these?’ So heasws said: ‘There is no problem with it except displayed ornaments and the displayed necklace’.
en.sarwar: 10. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Safwan from Hariz from ‘Amir ibn Judha‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about dyed cloths used by women in the state of Ihram. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful except ornaments visible on her front side as well as visible necklace.’”
